summary: Windows x86 crash (stack overflow) on .NET 8/9 caused by missing [UnmanagedCallConv] on LibraryImport declarations — all 878 P/Invoke calls default to StdCall instead of Cdecl on x86, causing stack corruption.

stepsToReproduce: Create a .NET 8 or .NET 9 project that references SkiaSharp 3.116+ (or 3.119.1)
Call any SkiaSharp P/Invoke method (e.g. SKBitmap.GetPixel)
reproEvidence.environmentDetails: Windows 11 (ARM and x64), .NET 8.0 or 9.0, SkiaSharp 3.116.0+ (also 3.119.1), x86 target only

versionAnalysis.relevanceReason: The LibraryImport declarations in SkiaApi.generated.cs still lack [UnmanagedCallConv] — all 878 P/Invoke calls are affected on Windows x86.

regression.reason: SkiaSharp 3.x introduced LibraryImport (USE_LIBRARY_IMPORT) for .NET 7+ targets. The generated code omits [UnmanagedCallConv(CallConvs = new[] { typeof(CallConvCdecl) })], defaulting to StdCall on Windows x86 instead of Cdecl. SkiaSharp 2.88.x used DllImport with explicit CallingConvention.Cdecl which worked correctly.

analysis.summary: The SkiaSharp code generator (utils/SkiaSharpGenerator/Generate/Generator.cs) produces [LibraryImport] declarations without [UnmanagedCallConv] for .NET 7+ targets. On Windows x86, LibraryImport defaults to StdCall (via Winapi), while the native C API (sk_types.h defines SK_C_API as dllexport/dllimport only, no calling convention) uses cdecl. This calling convention mismatch causes stack corruption on every P/Invoke call, resulting in a stack overflow. On x64, there is only one calling convention so the mismatch is harmless. On .NET Framework, the USE_DELEGATES path uses explicit CallingConvention.Cdecl via UnmanagedFunctionPointer, which is why net472+x86 works fine.
analysis.rationale: Classified as type/bug with critical severity: this is a regression that crashes 100% of x86 .NET 8/9 apps using SkiaSharp 3.x on Windows. Two independent reporters confirm the issue. Root cause is confirmed in the code generator — the DllImport path has CallingConvention.Cdecl but the LibraryImport path omits [UnmanagedCallConv]. The fix is in the generator, not the generated code.

keySignals.text: when we compile the application for x86 then we are getting app crash
keySignals.interpretation: x86-specific crash — calling convention mismatch is the classic cause since x64 has only one convention.

keySignals.text: When we rollback the SkiaSharp nuget to version 2.88.9 then DLLs loading from subdirectory also work as expected (x64, x86)
keySignals.interpretation: 2.88.x predates USE_LIBRARY_IMPORT, used DllImport with explicit Cdecl — confirms the regression is in the switch to LibraryImport.
Stack overflow from P/Invoke is the classic symptom of calling convention mismatch — StdCall callee pops args, Cdecl caller also tries to pop, corrupting the stack.

keySignals.text: x86 + net472: working / x64 + net472: working / x86 + Net8.0: ISSUE / x64 + Net8.0: working
keySignals.interpretation: Perfectly matches: net472 uses USE_DELEGATES (explicit Cdecl), net8.0 uses USE_LIBRARY_IMPORT (missing Cdecl), x64 is immune to convention mismatch.

codeInvestigation.finding: LibraryImport declarations are emitted without [UnmanagedCallConv(CallConvs = new[] { typeof(CallConvCdecl) })]. The DllImport fallback on the next line correctly specifies CallingConvention.Cdecl.
[LibraryImport(SKIA)] with no [UnmanagedCallConv] — defaults to platform calling convention (StdCall on Windows x86). 878 LibraryImport declarations are affected.
[DllImport(SKIA, CallingConvention = CallingConvention.Cdecl)] — the fallback path correctly specifies Cdecl. This path is used on older frameworks.
SK_C_API defines only visibility attributes (dllexport/dllimport), NOT calling conventions. Native functions use the C default: cdecl.
USE_LIBRARY_IMPORT is defined for net7.0+ targets. This activates the LibraryImport code path.

workarounds: Target x64 instead of x86 — x64 has only one calling convention, avoiding the mismatch
Use SkiaSharp 2.88.x which uses DllImport with explicit CallingConvention.Cdecl
Use .NET Framework (net472) target which activates the USE_DELEGATES path with explicit Cdecl
nextQuestions: Does the first reporter's 'DLLs from subdirectory' issue have the same root cause, or is it a separate native library resolution problem?
Are there any x86 test runners in CI that would catch this regression?

resolution.hypothesis: The code generator emits [LibraryImport] without [UnmanagedCallConv] for all P/Invoke declarations. On Windows x86, this defaults to StdCall instead of Cdecl, causing stack corruption on every native call.

proposals.title: Fix code generator to add [UnmanagedCallConv(CallConvs = new[] { typeof(CallConvCdecl) })]
proposals.description: Add the [UnmanagedCallConv] attribute to LibraryImport declarations in Generator.cs at line 546. Then regenerate all bindings with pwsh ./utils/generate.ps1. This fixes all 878 P/Invoke declarations at once.

proposals.title: Target x64 instead of x86
proposals.description: Build for x64 (or AnyCPU preferring 64-bit) instead of x86. On x64 Windows, there is only one calling convention so the mismatch has no effect.

resolution.recommendedProposal: Fix code generator to add [UnmanagedCallConv(CallConvs = new[] { typeof(CallConvCdecl) })]
resolution.recommendedReason: One-line fix in the generator that resolves the issue at the source. Regenerating bindings will fix all 878 declarations. The x64 workaround doesn't help users who must target x86.

actionability.reason: Root cause is identified in the code generator with high confidence. Needs a fix PR: update Generator.cs and regenerate bindings. Investigation should confirm with an x86 test.

actions.description: Labels are already correct: type/bug, area/SkiaSharp, os/Windows-Classic, tenet/reliability
Post analysis with root cause and workaround
actions.comment:
Thanks for the report, and thanks @TomPMoleman for the confirming stack trace — that narrowed this down significantly.

The root cause appears to be a calling convention mismatch on Windows x86. SkiaSharp 3.x uses `[LibraryImport]` for .NET 7+ targets, but the generated bindings don't include `[UnmanagedCallConv(CallConvs = new[] { typeof(CallConvCdecl) })]`. On x86 Windows, `LibraryImport` defaults to `StdCall`, while the native C API uses `cdecl`. On x64 there's only one calling convention, which is why it works there. On .NET Framework, a different code path with explicit `CallingConvention.Cdecl` is used, explaining why net472 is fine.

As a workaround, targeting x64 instead of x86 avoids the issue entirely. We'll work on a fix in the code generator.
